title: Understanding Angular Motion
text: To find the angle rotated through, we need to use the formula for angular displacement when angular acceleration is constant.

title: Formula for Angular Displacement
text: The formula for angular displacement \( $$\theta$$ \) when angular acceleration \( \alpha \) is constant is: \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \omega_0*t + 0.5*\alpha*t**2 \), where \( \omega_0 \) is the initial angular velocity.

title: Substitute Known Values
text: Substitute the known values into the formula: \( \omega_0 $$=$$ $$2.0$$ \) $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, \( \alpha $$=$$ $$1.0$$ \) rad/s², and \( $$t$$ $$=$$ $$5$$ \) s.
